subject: Physics
options: In frequency modulation the amplitude of the high frequency carrier wave is made to vary in proportion to the amplitude of the audio signal.
In frequency modulation the amplitude of the high frequency carrier wave is made to vary in proportion to the frequency of the audio signal.
In amplitude modulation the amplitude of the high frequency carrier wave is made to vary in proportion to the amplitude of the audio signal.
In amplitude modulation the frequency of the high frequency carrier wave is made to vary in proportion to the amplitude of the audio signal.
solution: As per properties of A.M. in amplitude modulation the amplitude of high frequency carrier wave is made to vary in proportion to the amplitude of the audio signal.

subject: Physics
options: Ground wave signals are more stable than the sky wave signals.
The critical frequency of an ionospheric layer is the highest frequency that will be reflected back by the layer when it is vertically incident.
Electromagnetic waves of frequencies higher than about $30 \mathrm{MHz}$ cannot penetrate the ionosphere.
Sky wave signals in the broadcast frequency range are stronger at night than in the day time.
solution: Above critical frequency $\left(f_c\right)$, an electromagnetic wave penetrates the ionosphere and is not reflected by it.

solution:
The range of a TV tower antenna is given by 

d=2hR

New height is given as

h'=1.21h 

Let the new range be

 d'=2h'R⇒d'=1.212hR=1.1d

 So, the increase in the range is =1.1d-dd×100=10%

solution: Maximum distance on earth where object can be detected is $\mathrm{d}$, then $\begin{array}{ll} & (h+R)^2=d^2+R^2 \\ \Rightarrow & d^2=h^2+2 R h \\ \text { since } & h < < R, \quad \Rightarrow d^2=2 h R \\ \Rightarrow & d=\sqrt{2(500)\left(6.4 \times 10^6\right)}=80 \mathrm{~km} \end{array}$

subject: Physics
solution:
AM broadcasting is radio broadcasting using amplitude modulation (AM) transmissions.

AM Broadcast range → 540–1600 kHz 

FM broadcasting is the method of radio broadcasting that uses frequency modulation (FM).

FM Broadcast range →88 – 108 MHz

Television →54 – 890 MHz

Satellite communication →3.7–4.2 GHz

∴A-II, B-I, C-IV, D-III

Height of transmitting antenna is, hT=50 m

Height of receiving antenna is, hR=32 m

dM=2RhT+2RhR where R is radius of the Earth,

dM=2RhT+2RhRdM=2×64×105×50+2×64×105×32dM=800010+640010dM=1440010 mdM=45536.7 mdM=45.5 km

subject: Physics
solution:
Modulating index is stated as μ=AmAc, where, Am=amplitude of modulating signal and Ac=amplitude of carrier signal.

Now, for first case, μ1=XY

For second case, μ2=X2Y

Thus, the ratio of μ1μ2=XYX2Y=21⇒μ1:μ2=2:1

subject: Physics
solution:
Linear antennas are considered to be those antennas that imply the use of electrically thin conductors having diameter much less than the wavelength of the electromagnetic wave passing through them.

The formula to calculate the effective power radiated by a linear antenna is given by

Peff=kl2λ2

where, k is a constant.
